# Fleet roster DTO: wake-state read producer ‚Äî on | off | suppressed | unknown (S2 axis 1, D#15249)

## Context

The first of the two named W2 wiring leaves from the graduated FM design-first record (D#15249 ‚Üí #15254): the wake axis of the S2 telltale taxonomy. The STEP_BACK verified at source (three independent sweeps: fleet-service grep, DTO assembly check, ai/-wide field-name sweep ‚Äî zero hits) that **no read/observe producer exists**: the daemon toggles are CONTROL state; the roster DTO carries no wake observation fields. Until this leaf lands, the telltale UI renders the axis as `unknown` ‚Äî the honest default the record mandates.

## The Problem

TODAY's lived incident: the operator hand-disabled the wake daemon and the cockpit showed nothing. #14537 (`setWakeEnabled`) is the WRITE verb ‚Äî a toggle without an observable state is a blind switch; the record explicitly rules #14537 write-adjacency, never the telltale's evidence source.

## The Architectural Reality

- The graduated taxonomy (binding): `wake: on | off | suppressed | unknown` ‚Äî one of two ORTHOGONAL axes (throttle is the sibling leaf). Honest-degradation rule: unknown renders as unknown, never as healthy.
- The producer feeds the roster/detail DTO assembly (the projection surface the cockpit binds) ‚Äî observation read from wherever wake-subscription truth lives (daemon state / wake-subscription liveness), never inferred from control intent.
- Placement per the D#15209 ledger default: wiring ‚Üí #13015.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The roster/detail DTO gains a wake-state observation field with exactly the four taxonomy values; absence of truth = `unknown` (fail-honest, never a default-to-on).
- [ ] The producer reads observation truth (subscription/daemon state), not control intent; the #14537 verb (when it lands) mutates state that THIS producer independently observes.
- [ ] Unit coverage: all four states + the unknown-on-unreachable path.
- [ ] The projection contract documented where the DTO assembly lives (Anchor & Echo).

## Out of Scope

The throttle axis (sibling leaf) ¬∑ the telltale UI (renders both axes; separate leaf under #14560) ¬∑ #14537 itself.

## Related

Epic #13015 (parent) ¬∑ D#15249 (graduated record, OQ-S2.3 amended resolution) ¬∑ #15254 (registry: the taxonomy table) ¬∑ #14537 (write adjacency).

Sweeps: latest-open checked 2026-07-16T15:05Z (latest #15267) ‚Äî no equivalent. A2A herd-window clean.

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-07-16T16:20:48Z

**Truth-source design decision (AC: the producer reads observation truth, documented).**

Named sources, all observation-side, verified in code:

1. **Subscription intent:** the WakeSubscriptionService-compatible read path (`list()` output), dependency-injected per the `fleetA2AActivityAdapter` sibling pattern ‚Äî callers keep ownership of identity binding; no singleton import.
2. **Daemon liveness:** the wake daemon's PID file (`daemon.mjs:364`, exclusive-create) + a `process.kill(pid, 0)` existence probe ‚Äî same-host by product definition. **Rejected alternative, for the record:** watermark/state-file mtime freshness ‚Äî the watermark advances only on delivery activity, so a quiet fleet is indistinguishable from a dead daemon by mtime; mtime-is-not-liveness.
3. **Mapping (the graduated taxonomy):** no subscription ‚Üí `off` (observed) ¬∑ subscription + pid-alive ‚Üí `on` ¬∑ subscription + pid-dead/absent ‚Üí `suppressed` (intent on, delivery off ‚Äî exactly today's operator incident) ¬∑ any source unreadable ‚Üí `unknown` with a degraded-capability envelope carrying the reason (the `fleetA2AActivityAdapter` envelope pattern, which already codifies the fail-honest discipline).

Shape: `ai/services/fleet/fleetWakeStateAdapter.mjs` (sibling-pattern fast-path per structural pre-flight), pure functions + injected readers, feeding `FleetManager.fleetRuntimeStatus()` rows. Control intent (#14537's realm) is never consulted. Implementation next on `grace/15271-wake-wiring-arc`. ‚Äî Grace üññ

- 2026-07-16T16:33:17Z @neo-opus-grace cross-referenced by PR #15280
- 2026-07-16T16:43:19Z @neo-opus-grace cross-referenced by PR #15281
- 2026-07-16T17:15:36Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `bb791ba` - "fix(fleet): wake producer repair ‚Äî the three-cluster review contract (#15271)

Cluster 1 (producer contract / projection / provenance): the trusted
Brain-side observation source lands as readActiveWakeSubscriptionIdentities
‚Äî one bulk scan of ACTIVE wake subscriptions under the fleet process's own
authority (administrative read; the caller-owner management API is never
impersonated), lazily importing the graph service per call so a fresh scan
serves every snapshot. devFleetServer (the production entrypoint) wires the
config-resolved daemon PID path + the scan into FleetManager.wakeStateOptions,
following the wireBootIdentityReadSource fail-soft precedent. fleetRoster()
joins the snapshot: createFleetCockpitStatus gains the wakeStatus dimension ‚Äî
per-row wake telltale state (the four-state contract, tri-state honest like
lifecycle), sources.wake, and the wake capability; a manager seam without the
producer reads not-wired, never guessed. The adapter gains the bulk-reader
seam (listActiveSubscriptionIdentities + wakeIdentityFor mapping, default
'@'+githubUsername per the mailbox-identity convention).

Cluster 2 (daemon identity): a responding PID is not the daemon ‚Äî the OS
recycles PIDs. Liveness now verifies process identity against the daemon's
launch marker via an injected command reader (default: ps -p <pid> -o
command=); a marker mismatch reads observed-dead-with-reuse named, an
unreadable identity reads unknown.

Cluster 3 (fail-honest diagnostics): failures are row-LOCAL by contract (the
shared-state bleed that let one agent's reader throw poison sibling reasons
is gone), reasons are redacted before any Body projection (secret vocabulary
+ token shapes masked), and out-of-contract reader values degrade the
CAPABILITY (partial, counted) so a broken reader can no longer hide under
wired/observed."
### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-07-16T17:16:03Z

**Contract Ledger backfill (cycle-1 RA-1, delivered at PR #15280 head `bb791bad5c`).**

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Behavior | Fallback | Diagnostics | Projection owner | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `readActiveWakeSubscriptionIdentities()` (new) | this leaf | one bulk scan of ACTIVE `WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION` nodes ‚Üí holder identities; administrative read under the fleet process's authority ‚Äî never the caller-owner API | any failure THROWS ‚Üí adapter degrades honestly | thrown reasons redacted at the adapter | Brain-only | spec: failing scan ‚Üí all-unknown + degraded |
| `fleetWakeStateAdapter.readFleetWakeStateSnapshot` | this leaf | subscription intent √ó daemon liveness ‚Üí four-state rows + capability envelope | missing sources ‚Üí `unknown` rows, `degraded/none|partial` capability | row-LOCAL, redacted, out-of-contract values counted into the capability | Brain-side producer | 22 adapter specs |
| daemon liveness | this leaf | PID file (exclusive-create) + `kill(pid,0)` + **process-identity marker match** (`ps -o command=`) | reuse ‚Üí observed-dead-named; unreadable identity ‚Üí unknown | bounded reasons | Brain | PID-reuse + identity-unreadable specs |
| `FleetManager.wakeStateOptions` / `fleetWakeStatus()` | this leaf | turnkey roster snapshot; options injected | no options ‚Üí all-unknown `degraded/none` | ‚Äî | Brain facade | 2 wiring specs |
| entrypoint wiring (`devFleetServer`) | this leaf | config-resolved PID path + the bulk scan injected before serving | fail-soft per the boot-identity precedent | ‚Äî | the process entrypoint | wiring diff |
| `fleetRoster()` + `createFleetCockpitStatus` | this leaf | rows gain `wake` (tri-state honest, like `lifecycle`) + `sources.wake` + `capabilities.wake` | producer absent ‚Üí `not-wired/none` | reasons ride the row only when `unknown` | Body pure map (hemisphere boundary holds) | bridge + cockpit specs |

Identity mapping seam: `wakeIdentityFor` default `'@' + (githubUsername ?? id)` ‚Äî the mailbox-identity convention; entrypoint-overridable. Known bound (stated at the scan module): cross-process graph reads serve the loaded projection ‚Äî a subscription created moments ago in another process may appear one refresh late; an eventual-consistency lag on a telltale, never a wrong `off`. ‚Äî Grace

- 2026-07-16T18:46:35Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `5e801d0` - "fix(fleet): wake producer exact-head repair ‚Äî config authority, durable truth, closed taxonomy (#15271)

Four exact-head falsifiers from the cross-family pre-review, each repaired
against the mechanism rather than the symptom.

1. devFleetServer crashed before serving. The entrypoint read the PID dir off
Tier-1 AiConfig, which carries only the flat wakeDaemonHeartbeatAlivePath leaf
‚Äî the wakeDaemon subtree is owned by the memory-core config. AiConfig.wakeDaemon
is undefined, so the deref threw a TypeError on boot. It now reads
memoryCoreConfig.wakeDaemon.dataDir, mirroring the wake daemon's own authority
(ai/daemons/wake/daemon.mjs). Per ADR-0019 the repair is to read the correct
leaf, NOT to guard the wrong one with a defensive optional chain (B3).

2. The bulk reader served process-local cache, not shared truth. It read
service.db straight off a lazy import ‚Äî db does not exist until initAsync()
resolves ‚Äî and then scanned the in-memory nodes.items projection. The fleet
server is a SEPARATE process from the MCP server that writes subscriptions, so
a cache read reports a subscribed agent as off: the exact blind-switch the S2
axis exists to catch. It now awaits initAsync() and queries durable SQLite,
mirroring the established WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION durable read in
WakeSubscriptionService minus its owner predicate (this reader is fleet-wide by
design). The cache scan survives only as an injected-double seam for tests.

3. The DTO leaked a wiring fact into an observation field. A row without a
producer read state:'not-wired' ‚Äî a fifth value in a taxonomy the ticket closes
over on|off|suppressed|unknown, which every consumer would have had to re-derive.
Absence is now unknown/none with a reason; the wiring axis stays where it
belongs, on sources.wake and the capability envelope.

4. The capability envelope mistook a reader for truth. A wholesale bulk-scan
failure left a throw-only function behind, and hasReader counted its mere
existence ‚Äî so a throwing scan plus an unreadable daemon reported partial
visibility while observing nothing. readerUsable now separates "a function
exists" from "it can answer", and confidence degrades to none.

Coverage lands where the defects lived and nowhere else: the bulk-reader module
had no spec at all (which is why the durable/init defects were probe-only
findings), and the cockpit DTO spec never mentioned wake (which is why the
not-wired leak was invisible). Both boundaries are now pinned, including the
closed-taxonomy invariant.

Evidence: 483/483 fleet unit specs green. The 11 new specs fail 9/9 against the
pre-repair source (red-then-green, not green-by-construction). Falsifier 1 is an
entrypoint defect no unit spec reaches, so it is verified by direct probe ‚Äî
AiConfig.wakeDaemon is undefined and throws, memoryCoreConfig.wakeDaemon.dataDir
resolves ‚Äî and by booting devFleetServer, which now serves {"ok":true,"result":[]}
instead of crashing.

The ticket Ledger stays in comment by reviewer request; the seam is unchanged for
the stacked throttle head."
- 2026-07-16T18:51:31Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `fc74f0f` - "fix(fleet): wait via ready(), never initAsync() ‚Äî the core.Base external-wait contract (#15271)

Cross-family re-review caught the durable-read repair reaching for the wrong
wait. `readActiveWakeSubscriptionIdentities` awaited `service.initAsync()`, but
`core.Base` triggers `initAsync()` itself during `Neo.create()` and warns in
terms: awaiting it externally executes it twice, a fatal-duplication class.
`ready()` is the sanctioned external wait, and the sibling consumer one file
over (`WakeSubscriptionService`) already does exactly that on this same
singleton ‚Äî the deviation was mine, not a missing pattern.

Worth naming why this survived a green suite: `GraphService.initAsync()` carries
its own idempotence guard, so the violation was absorbed rather than surfaced.
The guard defends a different concern; it is not the contract. So the spec
double now models `core.Base` honestly ‚Äî it exposes `ready()` only ‚Äî and a pin
asserts the reader never calls `initAsync()`, because a future author would
otherwise regress this in silence exactly the way it slipped through here.

Evidence: 484/484 fleet unit specs green."
- 2026-07-16T19:13:45Z @tobiu referenced in commit `5071deb` - "feat(fleet): wake-state read producer ‚Äî the S2 wake axis (#15271) (#15280)

* feat(fleet): wake-state read producer ‚Äî the S2 wake axis from pure observation (#15271)

fleetWakeStateAdapter maps wake-subscription intent x wake-daemon liveness
onto the graduated taxonomy (on | off | suppressed | unknown), sibling-
patterned on fleetA2AActivityAdapter: dependency-injected read paths (the
caller owns identity binding ‚Äî WSS.list() is caller-bound), a degraded-
capability envelope, bounded rows. Daemon liveness = the exclusive-create
PID file + a process.kill(pid, 0) probe (ESRCH = observed-dead stale file,
EPERM = alive); the mtime alternative is rejected at the module doc ‚Äî the
watermark only advances on delivery, so a quiet fleet would read as dead.
Control intent (setWakeEnabled) is never consulted. Config-entrypoint
discipline per ADR 0019: non-entrypoints resolve no config, so the PID
path + subscription reader arrive via the injected wakeStateOptions seam
and the honest default is every-row-unknown under degraded/none.

FleetManager.fleetWakeStatus() exposes the roster snapshot turnkey ‚Äî the
third fleet view beside repos and processes.

* fix(fleet): wake producer repair ‚Äî the three-cluster review contract (#15271)

Cluster 1 (producer contract / projection / provenance): the trusted
Brain-side observation source lands as readActiveWakeSubscriptionIdentities
‚Äî one bulk scan of ACTIVE wake subscriptions under the fleet process's own
authority (administrative read; the caller-owner management API is never
impersonated), lazily importing the graph service per call so a fresh scan
serves every snapshot. devFleetServer (the production entrypoint) wires the
config-resolved daemon PID path + the scan into FleetManager.wakeStateOptions,
following the wireBootIdentityReadSource fail-soft precedent. fleetRoster()
joins the snapshot: createFleetCockpitStatus gains the wakeStatus dimension ‚Äî
per-row wake telltale state (the four-state contract, tri-state honest like
lifecycle), sources.wake, and the wake capability; a manager seam without the
producer reads not-wired, never guessed. The adapter gains the bulk-reader
seam (listActiveSubscriptionIdentities + wakeIdentityFor mapping, default
'@'+githubUsername per the mailbox-identity convention).

Cluster 2 (daemon identity): a responding PID is not the daemon ‚Äî the OS
recycles PIDs. Liveness now verifies process identity against the daemon's
launch marker via an injected command reader (default: ps -p <pid> -o
command=); a marker mismatch reads observed-dead-with-reuse named, an
unreadable identity reads unknown.

Cluster 3 (fail-honest diagnostics): failures are row-LOCAL by contract (the
shared-state bleed that let one agent's reader throw poison sibling reasons
is gone), reasons are redacted before any Body projection (secret vocabulary
+ token shapes masked), and out-of-contract reader values degrade the
CAPABILITY (partial, counted) so a broken reader can no longer hide under
wired/observed.

* fix(fleet): wake producer exact-head repair ‚Äî config authority, durable truth, closed taxonomy (#15271)

Four exact-head falsifiers from the cross-family pre-review, each repaired
against the mechanism rather than the symptom.

1. devFleetServer crashed before serving. The entrypoint read the PID dir off
Tier-1 AiConfig, which carries only the flat wakeDaemonHeartbeatAlivePath leaf
‚Äî the wakeDaemon subtree is owned by the memory-core config. AiConfig.wakeDaemon
is undefined, so the deref threw a TypeError on boot. It now reads
memoryCoreConfig.wakeDaemon.dataDir, mirroring the wake daemon's own authority
(ai/daemons/wake/daemon.mjs). Per ADR-0019 the repair is to read the correct
leaf, NOT to guard the wrong one with a defensive optional chain (B3).

2. The bulk reader served process-local cache, not shared truth. It read
service.db straight off a lazy import ‚Äî db does not exist until initAsync()
resolves ‚Äî and then scanned the in-memory nodes.items projection. The fleet
server is a SEPARATE process from the MCP server that writes subscriptions, so
a cache read reports a subscribed agent as off: the exact blind-switch the S2
axis exists to catch. It now awaits initAsync() and queries durable SQLite,
mirroring the established WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION durable read in
WakeSubscriptionService minus its owner predicate (this reader is fleet-wide by
design). The cache scan survives only as an injected-double seam for tests.

3. The DTO leaked a wiring fact into an observation field. A row without a
producer read state:'not-wired' ‚Äî a fifth value in a taxonomy the ticket closes
over on|off|suppressed|unknown, which every consumer would have had to re-derive.
Absence is now unknown/none with a reason; the wiring axis stays where it
belongs, on sources.wake and the capability envelope.

4. The capability envelope mistook a reader for truth. A wholesale bulk-scan
failure left a throw-only function behind, and hasReader counted its mere
existence ‚Äî so a throwing scan plus an unreadable daemon reported partial
visibility while observing nothing. readerUsable now separates "a function
exists" from "it can answer", and confidence degrades to none.

Coverage lands where the defects lived and nowhere else: the bulk-reader module
had no spec at all (which is why the durable/init defects were probe-only
findings), and the cockpit DTO spec never mentioned wake (which is why the
not-wired leak was invisible). Both boundaries are now pinned, including the
closed-taxonomy invariant.

Evidence: 483/483 fleet unit specs green. The 11 new specs fail 9/9 against the
pre-repair source (red-then-green, not green-by-construction). Falsifier 1 is an
entrypoint defect no unit spec reaches, so it is verified by direct probe ‚Äî
AiConfig.wakeDaemon is undefined and throws, memoryCoreConfig.wakeDaemon.dataDir
resolves ‚Äî and by booting devFleetServer, which now serves {"ok":true,"result":[]}
instead of crashing.

The ticket Ledger stays in comment by reviewer request; the seam is unchanged for
the stacked throttle head.

* fix(fleet): wait via ready(), never initAsync() ‚Äî the core.Base external-wait contract (#15271)

Cross-family re-review caught the durable-read repair reaching for the wrong
wait. `readActiveWakeSubscriptionIdentities` awaited `service.initAsync()`, but
`core.Base` triggers `initAsync()` itself during `Neo.create()` and warns in
terms: awaiting it externally executes it twice, a fatal-duplication class.
`ready()` is the sanctioned external wait, and the sibling consumer one file
over (`WakeSubscriptionService`) already does exactly that on this same
singleton ‚Äî the deviation was mine, not a missing pattern.

Worth naming why this survived a green suite: `GraphService.initAsync()` carries
its own idempotence guard, so the violation was absorbed rather than surfaced.
The guard defends a different concern; it is not the contract. So the spec
double now models `core.Base` honestly ‚Äî it exposes `ready()` only ‚Äî and a pin
asserts the reader never calls `initAsync()`, because a future author would
otherwise regress this in silence exactly the way it slipped through here.

Evidence: 484/484 fleet unit specs green."
